Now we need to output JSON with files array. Each file object has word, filename, lines. Filenames: lowercase, spaces to underscores, hyphens stay. For "indeterminate" -> "indeterminate.md". "indetermination" -> "indetermination.md". "indicating" -> "indicating.md". "indigenous" -> "indigenous.md". "indirect" -> "indirect.md". "individual" -> "individual.md". "indoor" -> "indoor.md". "inducing" -> "inducing.md". "inductive" -> "inductive.md". "inevitable" -> "inevitable.md". No hyphens in these words.

Now we need to ensure that the lines are natural. Let's double-check each language.

For "indeterminate": German "Eine unbestimmte Tomate ist eine Pflanze." That's fine. Japanese "不定形のトマトは植物だ。" - "不定形" might be a bit technical but acceptable. Chinese "一个不确定的番茄是植物。" - "不确定的" is common. Good.

For "indetermination": German "Unbestimmtheit ist ein Zustand." - "Unbestimmtheit" is correct. Japanese "不確定性は状態だ。" - "不確定性" is fine. Chinese "不确定性是一种状态。" - good.

For "indicating": German "Anzeigen ist ein Signal." - "Anzeigen" as noun (das Anzeigen) but without article is okay. Japanese "示すことは信号だ。" - "示すこと" is gerund. Chinese "指示是一种信号。" - "指示" can be noun meaning instruction, but also verb. It's fine.

For "indigenous": We changed to plant. English "An indigenous plant is a species." German "Eine einheimische Pflanze ist eine Art." - "einheimisch" is common. Japanese "在来植物は種だ。" - "在来植物" is indigenous plant. Chinese "一种本土植物是一个物种。" - good.

For "indirect": English "An indirect route is a path." German "Ein indirekter Weg ist ein Pfad." - good. Japanese "間接的な道は小道だ。" - "小道" is path. Chinese "一条间接的路是一条小径。" - good.

For "individual": English "An individual is a person." German "Ein Individuum ist eine Person." - good. Japanese "個人は人だ。" - good. Chinese "一个个体是一个人。" - good.

For "indoor": English "An indoor plant is a plant." German "Eine Zimmerpflanze ist eine Pflanze." - good. Japanese "屋内植物は植物だ。" - good. Chinese "一盆室内植物是植物。" - "一盆" is a measure word for potted plants, but "室内植物" is fine. Could also say "一株室内植物" but "一盆" is common. Acceptable.

For "inducing": English "Inducing is a stimulation." German "Induzieren ist eine Stimulation." - "Induzieren" is infinitive noun. Japanese "誘導することは刺激だ。" - "誘導すること" is inducing. Chinese "诱导是一种刺激。" - good.

For "inductive": English "An inductive sensor is a device." German "Ein induktiver Sensor ist ein Gerät." - good. Japanese "誘導センサーは装置だ。" - "誘導センサー" is inductive sensor. Chinese "一个电感传感器是一个设备。" - "电感传感器" is common.

For "inevitable": English "An inevitable accident is an event." German "Ein unvermeidlicher Unfall ist ein Ereignis." - good. Japanese "避けられない事故は出来事だ。" - good. Chinese "一个不可避免的事故是一个事件。" - good.

All seem natural. Now produce JSON.

Note: The output must be exactly one JSON object with "files" array and "problems" empty array. No markdown fences. So we output raw JSON.
